text:
To determine how many miles farther Shandy drove than Matteo, we need to calculate the distance each person traveled and then compare the two distances.

First, let's calculate the distance Matteo traveled:
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} = \text{Speed}_{\text{Matteo}} \times \text{Time}_{\text{Matteo}} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} = 55 \text{ miles per hour} \times 4 \text{ hours} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} = 220 \text{ miles} \]

Next, let's calculate the distance Shandy traveled:
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} = \text{Speed}_{\text{Shandy}} \times \text{Time}_{\text{Shandy}} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} = 45 \text{ miles per hour} \times 10 \text{ hours} \]
\[ \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} = 450 \text{ miles} \]

Now, we find the difference in the distances traveled by Shandy and Matteo:
\[ \text{Difference} = \text{Distance}_{\text{Shandy}} - \text{Distance}_{\text{Matteo}} \]
\[ \text{Difference} = 450 \text{ miles} - 220 \text{ miles} \]
\[ \text{Difference} = 230 \text{ miles} \]

Therefore, Shandy drove 230 miles farther than Matteo.
